proposed_nodes.label: Ethics and Language
proposed_nodes.reason: Stevenson 1944; the developed statement of emotivism.

proposed_nodes.label: Fifteen Sermons
proposed_nodes.reason: Butler 1726; carries his refutation of psychological egoism.

proposed_nodes.label: G. W. F. Hegel
proposed_nodes.reason: Central German idealist; needed to flesh out the post-Kantian lineage.

proposed_nodes.label: In a Different Voice
proposed_nodes.reason: Gilligan 1982, the founding work of care ethics; would carry the INTRODUCED_IN edge.

proposed_nodes.label: Jean-Jacques Rousseau
proposed_nodes.reason: Canonical social contract theorist; The Social Contract (1762).

proposed_nodes.label: John Locke
proposed_nodes.reason: Canonical social contract theorist between Hobbes and Rousseau; needed for the contract lineage.

proposed_nodes.label: Jonathan Dancy
proposed_nodes.reason: The principal developer of moral particularism (Ethics Without Principles); needed for its DEVELOPED_BY edge.

proposed_nodes.label: Judith Jarvis Thomson
proposed_nodes.reason: Coined 'trolley problem' and produced its canonical variants (bystander, footbridge).

proposed_nodes.label: Practical Ethics
proposed_nodes.reason: Singer 1979; the standard applied-ethics text, anchor for Singer's positions.

proposed_nodes.label: The Language of Morals
proposed_nodes.reason: Hare 1952; the work that introduced universal prescriptivism.

proposed_nodes.label: Thomas Nagel
proposed_nodes.reason: Co-developer of the moral luck debate and major figure in ethics; his reply paper named the problem.
